data.description: MSCI Applies Interim Freeze on Certain Index Adjustments for Indonesian Stocks

data.title: MegazoneCloud and Hana Bank in strategic partnership to develop digital financial services, focusing on AI and cloud.
data.description: In South Korea, a strategic alliance has been formed for the joint creation of cutting-edge financial solutions utilizing AI, cloud computing, and data science. The collaboration focuses on enhancing trade finance services, particularly for the importation of high-volume GPU and NPU servers. This initiative is in line with the government's ambitious Global Top 3 AI project.

data.title: NTSB blames FAA for multiple failures in D.C. crash due to poorly designed routes and ignored risks, causing midair collision last year.
data.description: A recent investigation by the National Transportation Safety Board revealed that the midair collision last year was caused by a combination of factors. The primary causes identified were inadequately designed flight routes and disregarded warnings regarding potential risks. This incident resulted in a total of 25 casualties and 14 injuries. The NTSB report highlighted the importance of addressing these issues to prevent similar accidents in the future.

data.title: Arcos and OverIT announce integration to streamline utility field operations, correction made on 27-Jan-2026.
data.description: Arcos and OverIT have recently forged an integration and distribution partnership to enhance efficiency in utility field operations. The news was disclosed on 27-Jan-2026 through PR Newswire. This collaboration aims to streamline operations and improve service delivery in the utility sector.

data.title: Osisko Development sells San Antonio Gold Project in Mexico to Axo Copper Corp, completing the transaction of Sapuchi Mexico.
data.description: In Montreal on Jan. 27, 2026, Osisko Development Corp. (NYSE: ODV, TSXV: ODV) has successfully finalized the sale of its entire ownership stake in the San Antonio Gold Project situated in Sonora State, Mexico to Axo Copper Corp. The sale was completed through the transfer of all shares of Sapuchi Minera S. de R.L. de C.V. Important to note, Osisko Development Corp. is collectively known as Osisko Development, marking an important milestone for the Company.
